description_long: This cowshed is a rectangular, two-story agricultural building with a concrete base and a white plastered upper section. The roof is a steeply pitched gable style, covered in weathered, corrugated metal sheets with visible moss and aging. The front facade features two shuttered windows and a large, red, single-panel garage-style door at ground level, while the rear has an external staircase leading to a raised entry door. Wooden shutters and trim accent the windows, and metal gutters run along the roof edges for drainage. The structure is robust, with utilitarian design elements typical of rural or peri-urban agricultural buildings. The overall appearance suggests durability and functionality, suitable for housing livestock or storing agricultural equipment.
description: A sturdy, two-story cowshed with a concrete base, white plastered walls, and a weathered gable metal roof, featuring shuttered windows and a large front door.
description_view_0: Front view showing two shuttered windows, a large red garage-style door, and the steep gable roof.
description_view_1: Side view with the long roof slope, external staircase, and a raised entry door, plus small rectangular windows near the base.
description_view_2: Rear view with two shuttered windows, visible gutters, and the gable roof peak.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with a single open shuttered window, long roof slope, and plain concrete base.
attribute_door_style: single-panel metal garage door with a plain design
attribute_window_presence: four rectangular windows with wooden shutters, two on each gable end
